steps.action: The term forró (Brazilian Portuguese pronunciati with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.
steps.text: The term forró (Brazilian Portuguese pronunciati with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.

steps.action: It encompasses various dance types as well as a
steps.text: It encompasses various dance types as well as a number of different musical genres.

steps.action: Their music genres and dances have gained widesp
steps.text: Their music genres and dances have gained widespread popularity in all regions of Brazil, especially during the Brazilian June Festivals.

steps.action: Forró encompasses various rural rhythms from sev
steps.text: Forró encompasses various rural rhythms from several northeastern states, such as baião, xote, arrasta pé, xaxado, coco, rural samba, among many other rhythms.

steps.action: The popular dances, according to the Pernambucan
steps.text: The popular dances, according to the Pernambucan press, were known as "forrobodó" or "forrobodança" or even "forrobodão" already by the end of the 19th century.

steps.action: The dance however becomes very different as you
steps.text: The dance however becomes very different as you cross the borders of the Northeast into the Southeast.

steps.action: The dance known as college forró is the most com
steps.text: The dance known as college forró is the most common style between the middle-class students of colleges and universities in the Southeast, having influences of other dances like salsa and samba-rock.

steps.action: The combination of triangle with accordion is a
steps.text: The combination of triangle with accordion is a combination that has already existed in European folk music before and is also used in Cajun music in the United States.

steps.action: The combination of zabumba and triangle is almos
steps.text: The combination of zabumba and triangle is almost always part of the rhythm section of any forró group.

steps.action: The triangle keeps an ongoing pulse on all the s
steps.text: The triangle keeps an ongoing pulse on all the sixteenth notes of the 44 beat, while accentuating the third sixteenth.

steps.action: The zabumba, which is played on both sides, on o
steps.text: The zabumba, which is played on both sides, on one side giving a grave sound and on the other a sharp whip-like sound, plays the syncopated rhythms essential to forró.

steps.action: The North-eastern scale represents the basis of
steps.text: The North-eastern scale represents the basis of a large part of the more traditional forró and the forró pé-de-serra, similar to the way the blues scale is the basis for the music of the Mississippi Delta.

steps.action: In the following decades this new kind of forró
steps.text: In the following decades this new kind of forró became much more popular in Northeastern Brazil than "traditional" forró.

steps.action: Amongst these there are many styles of dancing,
steps.text: Amongst these there are many styles of dancing, which varies from region to region, and may be known by different names according to the location.

steps.action: There are two dance roles, one of the leader and
steps.text: There are two dance roles, one of the leader and one of the follower.

steps.action: Especially in European forró communities, there
steps.text: Especially in European forró communities, there is a trend to break and discuss the traditional gender roles of leading men and following women.

steps.action: Unlike many other social dances it becomes more
steps.text: Unlike many other social dances it becomes more and more common to see same-sex couples on the dance floor or leading women and following men.

steps.action: Influences from Cuban salsa, Samba de Gafieira a
steps.text: Influences from Cuban salsa, Samba de Gafieira and zouk has given mobility to forró, with the follower— and occasionally the leader— being spun, although it's not essential to spin at all.

steps.action: The more complex movements may prove impossible
steps.text: The more complex movements may prove impossible to be executed in the usually crowded dancing area of forrós.

steps.action: It contains many variations of movements; Miudin
steps.text: It contains many variations of movements; Miudinho: the leader dances with their left side slightly tilted, their left hand on the follower's waist and both the follower's hands around the leader's neck.

steps.action: Some people like to include brega/calypso in the
steps.text: Some people like to include brega/calypso in the forró category, because this dance has suffered much influence of forró throughout the decades, but it's danced to its own rhythm (not to be confused with calypso music).

steps.action: Nordestino forró is danced with the couple much
steps.text: Nordestino forró is danced with the couple much closer together, with their legs often inter-twined and a characteristic sideways shuffle movement.

steps.action: Because of the intimacy, there are not as many s
steps.text: Because of the intimacy, there are not as many step variations in this style.

steps.action: Its basic step is forward-backwards — slightly s
steps.text: Its basic step is forward-backwards — slightly similar to traditional bolero or salsa in line.
